title: SUNCO OPS-SPEC SPEC-BLOCK
description: Schema for the YAML SPEC-BLOCK fenced inside OPS-SPEC.md produced by backend-phase-ops (Phase 46/M3.5). Per spec R2 (SUNCO:SPEC-BLOCK deterministic extraction) and BS1 (schema versioning). Lenient additive: listed fields required, additionalProperties permitted so downstream phases (Phase 47 backend-review-ops) may extend without schema bump. slo field is a structural projection of BACKEND-CONTEXT.md SLO section, NOT a verbatim duplicate — BACKEND-CONTEXT remains the source of truth for SLO intent.

version.description: Schema version per BS1. Constant 1 for Phase 46 output. Future schema bumps increment this and ship coordinated schema migrations.

deployment_topology.description: Deployment shape — single-region / multi-region (active-active or active-passive) / edge; rollout strategy (blue-green / canary / rolling); oncall topology. Object form preferred; string form acceptable for simple shapes.

observability.description: Observability posture across logs, metrics, and traces. Sub-objects required: logs, metrics, traces. Optional alerts array with per-alert name + threshold.

metrics.description: Required dimensions + SLI list + aggregation windows.

alerts.description: Alert inventory. Each alert has a name + threshold expression; runbook pointer optional (top-level runbook[] carries the action text).

runbook.description: Optional runbook entries — alert → action mapping. Each entry has alert (matching observability.alerts[].name) + action (prose instruction).

slo.description: Structural projection of BACKEND-CONTEXT SLO section. availability as n-nines string (e.g., '99.9%'); latency_p95_ms as integer milliseconds. MUST NOT verbatim-duplicate BACKEND-CONTEXT prose — projection only; BACKEND-CONTEXT remains source of truth.

latency_p99_ms.description: Optional p99 latency budget in milliseconds.

error_budget_policy.description: Optional error-budget policy — burn rate threshold, freeze criteria, alerting pointer. Prose acceptable.

anti_pattern_watchlist.description: Phase 42 backend-excellence anti-patterns this ops surface must avoid. Done-when (Phase 46) requires at least 3 entries, each citing a Phase 42 reference file (e.g., observability-and-operations.md / reliability-and-failure-modes.md). Prefer anti-patterns labeled 'Detection: deterministic candidate' that Phase 43 detector covers (missing-timeout, swallowed-catch, logged-secret).
